resident_memory_admission.change: limitations now describe reduced chunk capacity as a selected runtime constraint passed to native chunked matrix-warp dispatch

implementation.runtime_behavior: artifact-only contract propagation; no change to CUDA pixel math, matrix fitting, interpolation, rejection, integration, native allocation, or default dispatch

devices.name: NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Workstation Edition

assertions: reduced-capacity admission still selects chunk capacity 4 for the constrained fixture
limitations include native chunked matrix-warp dispatch wording
limitations no longer contain allocator-driven wording
default triangle warp batch remains chunked
native max chunk capacity is 0 for the unforced default path

real_200_light_baseline.reason: C: drive had about 0.369 GiB free during validation; this gate only changes artifact contract fields and admission wording.
real_200_light_baseline.input: M38 H-alpha, 200 lights, 20 bias, 20 dark, 20 flat

notes: No official PixInsight WBPP/PJSR source was read or used.
WBPP evidence remains limited to user-generated black-box timing/output metadata from earlier accepted baseline artifacts.
Input image directories were not modified.

known_limitations: This gate makes native capacity evidence auditable but does not reduce per-chunk launch overhead.
A new real 200-light run was skipped because C: free space was too low for a large output directory.
The next substantive optimization should target resident registration/warp orchestration or DQ/mask default-path behavior.
next_gate_recommendation: Implement a performance gate that reduces resident registration/warp per-chunk orchestration overhead, or a DQ/mask gate that moves source-DQ closer to the default resident path.
